ground_truth.title: Top 5 Home Renovations with the Highest ROI in 2025
ground_truth.main_content:
Not all upgrades are created equal. While it's easy to get caught up in trendy finishes or luxury features, smart property managers and investors focus on one thing: return on investment. If you're renovating to increase property value, attract higher-paying tenants, or prep for a future sale, you need to know which upgrades truly pay off.

At DOCI, we've tracked the data and seen the results firsthand. These five renovations consistently offer the highest ROI in 2025 and they're well worth the investment.

1. Steel Entry Door Replacement ROI: Up to 188%. Replacing the front door might seem like a small update, but it consistently ranks as one of the best-performing renovations in terms of ROI. A new steel entry door can return nearly double its cost up to 188% while improving security, aesthetics, and energy performance.

2. Garage Door Replacement ROI: Up to 194%. An outdated or dented garage door drags down your property's visual appeal. Replacing it with a modern, insulated model not only upgrades the look of your entire exterior but can also improve energy efficiency in attached garages or ADUs.

3. Manufactured Stone Veneer ROI: Up to 153%. You don't need to build with solid stone to get the luxury look. Manufactured stone veneer is a lightweight, cost-effective alternative that can dramatically elevate your building's exterior.

4. Minor Kitchen Remodel ROI: Approximately 96%. Kitchens are a deciding factor for both renters and buyers, but a full gut renovation isn't always necessary. A minor kitchen remodel typically defined as cosmetic updates like cabinet refacing, new counters, and updated hardware offers a strong ROI while minimizing disruption and cost.

5. Vinyl Siding Replacement ROI: Around 73%. While the ROI isn't as high as the other upgrades on this list, replacing old vinyl siding still delivers solid returns and major property value benefits. New siding improves curb appeal, energy efficiency, and protection against the elements.

How to Decide Which Renovations to Prioritize: If your budget doesn't allow for all five of these upgrades at once, focus on the projects that best fit your property's current condition, target renter profile, and long-term strategy.

At DOCI, our renovation strategies are built on one simple goal: get the most value from every dollar spent. We don't chase trends or recommend upgrades just for looks. Instead, we prioritize smart investments that increase rent without overcapitalizing, reduce turnover by improving tenant satisfaction, and protect long-term property value.
